summary_one_line: IHDR color_type/bit_depth bytes must form an individually-valid but mutually-disallowed combination (e.g. PALETTE with depth>8, or RGB/GA/RGBA with depth<8)

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The IHDR bit_depth byte (offset 0x18) and color_type byte (offset 0x19) must each pass the prior validity gates (L2622 valid bit_depth set, L2629 valid color_type set) yet form a forbidden pairing: color_type==3 (PALETTE) with bit_depth>8, OR color_type in {2,4,6} with bit_depth<8.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog discovers seeds whose color_type/bit_depth bytes settle on the specific small-integer comparands the L2636 disjuncts test against; the winner takes the TRUE side at L2636 (T=45 F=0) and resolves on all 10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive only reaches the FALSE side at L2636 (T=0 F=17) and stays blocked on 9/10 trials; its color_type/bit_depth byte combinations never hit a valid-but-forbidden pairing because random byte mutation rarely lands exactly on the enum comparands.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recording the operands of the color_type==N and bit_depth comparisons at L2636/L2622/L2629, finds the matching input byte at offset 0x18/0x19 and substitutes the other operand, planting the exact enum value needed to enter the forbidden combination.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same input feature: a valid-but-disallowed color_type/bit_depth combination at offsets 0x18/0x19 that drives L2636 to its TRUE side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves on all 10 trials and takes TRUE at L2636; its CMP_MAP distance feedback rewards inputs whose color_type/bit_depth bytes get progressively closer to the disallowed comparand values, building the combination by gradient.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive lacks CMP_MAP feedback, so an input that is one byte away from a forbidden combination earns no new coverage and is not retained; naive stays blocked 9/10 trials with L2636 never reaching TRUE.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/distance bucket for each integer CMP (color_type==N, bit_depth comparisons); an input whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus, letting the fuzzer climb toward the exact forbidden color_type/bit_depth pairing.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same valid-but-disallowed color_type/bit_depth combination at offsets 0x18/0x19 reaching L2636 TRUE; no extra byte requirement beyond naive's.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves on all 10 trials; its context-indexed edge map records OSS_FUZZ_png_check_IHDR edges under distinct call contexts, retaining more intermediate IHDR-parsing inputs and preserving the corpus diversity that eventually produces the forbidden combination.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive collapses all call paths into bare edges, so context-distinct IHDR explorations dedup to the same bucket and are discarded; it stays blocked 9/10 trials and never assembles the combination.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same IHDR-validation edge is new coverage under a new call context; this finer feedback granularity preserves more partial inputs and increases the chance of stumbling onto the disallowed color_type/bit_depth bytes.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the TRUE side of the L2636 color_type/bit_depth combination check.
The TRUE side fires for PALETTE+depth>8 or for RGB/GRAY_ALPHA/RGB_ALPHA with depth<8.
Winners take L2636 TRUE (T=45) while losers never do (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=45 F=0  T=0 F=17  if (((color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) && bit_depth ...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: color_type and bit_depth pass their individual validity gates at L2622 and L2629 before reaching L2636.
cmplog wins over naive with the I2S delta, resolving 10/10 vs 1/10.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes one logged CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile wins over naive with the value_profile delta.
value_profile preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching as new CMP_MAP buckets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins over naive with the ctx_coverage delta.
naive_ctx's CtxHook records the same edge at different map slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it

evidence_trail.claim: The discriminating bytes are at offsets 0x18 (bit_depth) and 0x19 (color_type) where W and L differ.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3864 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed <id>` for three cmplog winner seeds (14ddd5a497528d88, 0cca8cf8bb43c0be, 0ad471f0b6ede573); each chain contains an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depths 3, 11, and 10 respectively, all tracing to common ancestor 94c47bff5687a0c9 — direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find on the cmplog winner side.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a valid-but-disallowed color_type/bit_depth pairing where cmplog, value_profile, and naive_ctx all fail to outperform naive (no resolution advantage) would refute that these three techniques each enable the forbidden-combination branch.

weakest_evidence_point: The naive_ctx (ctx_coverage) advantage is the least mechanistically tied to the byte feature — its win may reflect general corpus-retention diversity rather than anything specific to the color_type/bit_depth combination, and its hit_div is only 7 vs 144/103 for the CMP techniques.
